# Integration Testing with Temporal CLI Dev Server

applyTo: "**/integration-tests/**,**/build.gradle"

---

## Overview

Integration tests run against a Temporal CLI dev server (`temporal server start-dev`), managed by Gradle. This provides a full-featured Temporal server with SQLite-based visibility, supporting all APIs including `ListWorkflowExecutions` and custom search attributes needed for correlation.

## Current Implementation

### 1. Temporal CLI Dev Server (Root build.gradle)

The root `build.gradle` manages the Temporal CLI dev server lifecycle with two tasks:

**Prerequisite**: The `temporal` CLI must be installed and available in PATH:
- macOS: `brew install temporal`
- Linux: `curl -sSf https://temporal.download/cli | sh`
- Windows: Download from https://github.com/temporalio/cli/releases
- CI: Installed via workflow step (see `.github/workflows/`)

#### startSharedTestServer
Starts the dev server for both unit and integration tests:
1. Checks if an external Temporal server is already running on port 7233 (useful for debugging)
2. If not, runs `temporal server start-dev --headless --db-filename <path> --port 7233`
3. Writes PID file for cleanup
4. Waits up to 30 seconds for server readiness
5. Generates `tests/Config.toml` for both `ballerina/tests/` and `integration-tests/tests/`

#### stopSharedTestServer
Stops the dev server using the saved PID file. Also registered via `gradle.buildFinished` to ensure cleanup even on failure.

### 2. Integration Tests Module (integration-tests)

#### Structure
```text
integration-tests/
├── Ballerina.toml           # Test package configuration
├── Dependencies.toml        # Dependencies (workflow module)
├── *.bal                    # Workflow definitions
├── tests/
│   ├── Config.toml          # Generated by Gradle with server URL
│   └── *_test.bal           # Test files
└── build.gradle             # Gradle test lifecycle management
```

#### Config.toml (Generated by startSharedTestServer)
```toml
[ballerina.workflow.workflowConfig]
mode = "LOCAL"
url = "localhost:7233"
namespace = "default"

[ballerina.workflow.workflowConfig.scheduler]
taskQueue = "BALLERINA_WORKFLOW_TASK_QUEUE"
maxConcurrentWorkflows = 100
maxConcurrentActivities = 100
```

#### integration-tests/build.gradle
Key aspects:
- Uses distribution-based testing via `io.ballerina.plugin`
- Sets `BALLERINA_DEV_COMPILE_BALLERINA_ORG=true` environment variable
- Uses `--offline` flag (bala is pre-installed in distribution)
- `ballerinaTest` task depends on `:startSharedTestServer`
- Parses `target/report/test_results.json` after test execution
- Throws `GradleException` if any tests failed (Ballerina may exit 0 even with failures)

### 3. Test Lifecycle Sequence

```text
1. Gradle Test Invocation
   └─> ./gradlew :workflow-integration-tests:test

2. Dependencies Resolution
   ├─> :workflow-ballerina:build (builds bala, installs to distribution)
   └─> :workflow-compiler-plugin:build

3. startSharedTestServer Task (requires `temporal` CLI in PATH)
   ├─> Checks for external server on port 7233
   ├─> Starts: temporal server start-dev --headless --db-filename <path>
   ├─> Waits for port 7233 to accept connections
   ├─> Saves PID to build/test-server/test-server.pid
   └─> Generates tests/Config.toml with server URL

4. ballerinaTest Task
   ├─> Executes: bal test --offline in integration-tests/
   ├─> Ballerina reads Config.toml (url = localhost:7233)
   ├─> Module init() connects to dev server
   ├─> Tests execute workflows against dev server
   ├─> Parses test_results.json for failures
   └─> Throws GradleException if any tests failed

5. stopSharedTestServer (buildFinished hook)
   ├─> Reads PID from build/test-server/test-server.pid
   ├─> Destroys server process via ProcessHandle
   └─> Deletes PID file
```

### 4. External Server Support

For debugging, you can run a Temporal server externally before running tests:
```bash
# Start server manually (tests will detect and use it)
temporal server start-dev --port 7233

# Run tests (will skip starting its own server)
./gradlew :workflow-integration-tests:test
```

The `startSharedTestServer` task checks port 7233 first. If a server is already running, it uses that server and skips starting/stopping its own.

## Test Categories

### Unit Tests (ballerina/tests/)
- No Temporal server needed for basic registration tests
- Tests registration, introspection, type conversions
- All processes registered once in `@test:BeforeSuite`

### Integration Tests (integration-tests/tests/)
- Uses Temporal CLI dev server
- Tests full workflow execution, activities, signals, correlation
- Supports `ListWorkflowExecutions` for correlation-based signal routing
- Includes HTTP activity tests (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, auth, XML responses)
- Includes builtin activity tests (sleep, currentTime, isReplaying, getWorkflowId, getWorkflowType)
- See [integration-tests/tests/](../../integration-tests/tests/) for test examples using `workflow:run()`, `workflow:sendData()`, and `workflow:getWorkflowResult()`

### Compiler Plugin Tests (compiler-plugin-tests/)
- Tests compile-time validations
- No Temporal server needed
- Tests WORKFLOW_107, WORKFLOW_108, WORKFLOW_113, and other error codes

## Test Commands

```bash
# Run integration tests (auto-starts Temporal CLI dev server)
./gradlew :workflow-integration-tests:test

# Run unit tests only
./gradlew :workflow-ballerina:test

# Run compiler plugin tests
./gradlew :workflow-compiler-plugin-tests:test

# Run all tests
./gradlew test
```

## Success Criteria

- Temporal CLI downloaded or found in PATH
- Server starts on port 7233 within 30 seconds
- SQLite-based visibility supports ListWorkflowExecutions
- Custom search attributes work for correlation
- Server stops cleanly after tests (even on failure)
- Integration tests pass with full Visibility API support
- Build fails when tests fail (via test_results.json parsing)
- External server detected and reused when available
- No orphaned server processes after build
- Config.toml generated correctly for both test suites
